rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK SPENCER III VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 12, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s negligence and strict liability causes of action are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. On the substantial factor causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden and Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether exposure to Square D asbestos-containing products was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related disease. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Dr. Bruch are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RONNIE CAMERON VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 12, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Cooper Industries LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration, defendant¿s responses to General Order No. 129 interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Mr. Wilson create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Chico X damming material) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PRATT, AVIS INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTER et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Plaintiffs Objection To Defendant Caesars Entertainment Corporations Request For Entry Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 12, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Hearing on plaintiffs¿ objection to specially appearing defendant Caesars Entertainment Corp.¿s proposed judgment: the court will not sign the judgment. The court granted Caesars¿ motion to quash and to dismiss action for lack of personal jurisdiction. Therefore this court does not have jurisdiction to enter judgment in favor of Caesars. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
